mission_and_vision.mission_statement: The mission of Scouting America is to prepare young people to make ethical and moral choices over their lifetimes by instilling in them the values of the Scout Oath and Law.
mission_and_vision.vision_statement: Scouting America will prepare every eligible youth in America to become a responsible, participating citizen and leader who is guided by the Scout Oath and Law.
mission_and_vision.scout_oath: Duty to God and country, duty to others, duty to self
mission_and_vision.scout_law: A Scout is Trustworthy, Loyal, Helpful, Friendly, Courteous, Kind, Obedient, Cheerful, Thrifty, Brave, Clean, and Reverent

core_pillars_of_summer_camp.Physical: Camp helps develop habits of healthy living through balanced meals and supervised physical activities.
core_pillars_of_summer_camp.Mental: Advancement programs offer challenges that build self-sufficiency and responsibility.
core_pillars_of_summer_camp.Social: The community of a summer camp helps build strong, morally rich people skills.
core_pillars_of_summer_camp.Spiritual: Spiritually rich time in nature helps develop a deeper understanding of a scout’s place in the universe.

what_makes_a_staff.Appearance: Hygiene, uniform, and how you present yourself make an impression. Before you speak, your appearance will have spoken.
what_makes_a_staff.Attitude: Stay positive and remember you are there to serve as well as have fun.
what_makes_a_staff.Personality: Patience, friendliness, humor, respect and enthusiasm; these traits make all the difference.
what_makes_a_staff.Knowledge: When teaching a Merit Badge or skill, the staff member should be an authority on the subject that he or she is teaching.

chain_of_command.Camp Director: Prime authority in camp. Runs the camp, ensuring safety, efficiency, budget, and compliance.
chain_of_command.Program Director: In charge of the camp experience (on stage).

NCS or equivalent training: First Year Camper Program / Model Camp (optional)

duties: Attend all staff meetings, trainings, and Staff Week.
Engage in these meetings and trainings, offering your ideas.
Be early to things, not just on time.
Participate in and assist with all camp-wide programs.
Be courteous and respectful to everyone.
Keep your appearance presentable and respectable.
Be in appropriate uniform for all activities.
Maintain your readiness and willingness to perform your duties by eating properly and getting adequate sleep.
Keep your staff quarters clean, orderly, and comfortable.
Take proper care of all camp equipment and facilities.
Take personal responsibility for the success of the entire camp.
Name Tags must be visible at all times.
Abide by the Scout Oath, Law, and ALL of the ideals of scouting.

the_rules.Phones: Keep it put away. Tablets and laptops as well.
the_rules.Photography and social media: Run any content you intend to post to social media by the Camp Director.
the_rules.Video Games and other recreation: Must be in line with scout values (No M rated games, R rated movies, etc.).
the_rules.Fraternization: Keep physical relationships out of camp.

stress_management.1. Work the Problem: Run a self-diagnostic: Are you dehydrated? Fueled? Sleep-deprived?
stress_management.2. Use Your Siesta: Go to your cabin, lie down, and relax for an hour.
stress_management.3. Manage Sensory Overload: Find a quiet space out of sight of the campers where you can safely decompress.
stress_management.4. Tag Out (The Strategic Retreat): Tell your Area Director you need five minutes.
stress_management.5. Call in the Grown-Ups: Go to an adult staff member that you trust if your mental health is slipping.

glossary.The Logs: The bleacher seating in front of the Dining Hall.
glossary.Troop Friend: Youth staff assigned to act as a liaison and friend to assigned troops.
glossary.Staff Hill: The hill on the north end of camp designated for Staff living facilities.
glossary.Smellables: Anything with an odor or scent that might attract wildlife.

this_is_your_life_schedule.Sunday: Report by 12pm, Class A uniform, troop check-in, camp tour, flags, dinner, Scoutmaster meeting.
this_is_your_life_schedule.Daily Life: 6am Eagle Trail (optional), Flags, Breakfast, KP duty, Program (9-11:50), Lunch/Siesta, Program (2-5), Flags, Dinner.
this_is_your_life_schedule.Saturday: Truncated flag ceremony, breakfast, campsite inspections, camp meeting, staff dismissed.

program_areas.The Area Director: Responsible for the upkeep and operation of his/her area.
program_areas.Inventory: Keep accurate track of inventory. Opening and closing inventory required.
program_areas.Safety: Carefully examine program area for potential hazards.
program_areas.Instructors: Achieve and maintain control of groups without physical punishment.

EDGE.Explain: Tell them what you’re gonna tell them.
EDGE.Demonstrate: Show them how to do it.
EDGE.Guide: Walk them through it, letting them try it a couple times.
EDGE.Enable: Test and give them the tools to go on.

teaching_methods.Game Design Method: An alternative method utilizing video game-like onboarding (Explain, Demonstrate, Guide, Enable via active engagement).

Guidance: No references to undergarments, nudity or bodily functions
No derogatory references to or stereotyping
No portrayal of sensitive social issues
Do not change lyrics to patriotic songs or hymns
No bathroom humor or water skits
All material should be vetted by the Program Director.

severe_weather_preparedness.Lightning Safety & The 30/30 Rule: When Thunder Roars, Go Indoors. Suspend activities if flash to bang is <= 30 seconds. Wait 30 minutes after last thunder.
severe_weather_preparedness.Heat & Thermal Stress Mitigation: Hydrate. Watch for Heat Exhaustion and Heatstroke.
severe_weather_preparedness.High Winds & Hazard Trees: Suspend climbing/range in high winds. Avoid dead trees (Widowmakers) and stay clear of fall radius.
severe_weather_preparedness.Camp Emergency Action Plans (EAP): Continuous bell means report to the parade grounds (flags).

Privacy and Accommodations: Separate Accommodations for youth/adults and male/female
Respect of Privacy (No cameras in changing areas)
Incident Response and Mandatory Reportin: Mandatory Reporters: Arizona State Law requires reporting suspected abuse to DCS (1-888-SOS-CHILD).

the_camp_lawton_guidelines: The Camper's Covenant (No weapons, tobacco, alcohol, drugs)

health_and_safety.Missing Person Protocol: Gather details, call 'Code Blue' over radio, centralized search by Camp Director.
health_and_safety.Emergency Alarm Procedures: Continuous dining hall bell. Secure hazards, route directly to Parade Grounds for headcount.
health_and_safety.Bear and Wildlife Safety: Smellables Policy (secure in Bear Box). Call 'Code Brown' for bear sightings.
health_and_safety.Fire Safety: Report wildfires immediately. Monitor distant fires. Evacuate if alarm sounds.
health_and_safety.Fatality Protocol: Secure area, clear onlookers, touch nothing, hand over to leadership.
health_and_safety.Active Shooters: Hide, seek shelter, flee, or fight back directly if necessary.

legal_policies.At Will Employment: Employment can be terminated at any time by either party.
legal_policies.Equal Employment Opportunity: Hiring based on qualifications without regard to protected characteristics.
legal_policies.Employee Grievances: Raise concerns with Area Directors, then submit written grievance to Camp Director if unresolved.
legal_policies.Anti-Retaliation and Whistleblower Polic: Protection for making good faith complaints regarding violations of law.

camp_opening_procedures.3-Year Activity Review Cycle: Review all camp activities and programs at least every three years.
camp_opening_procedures.Forest Service Permitting: Coordinate facility work, acquire permits, adhere to lease terms.
camp_opening_procedures.Pre-Camp Training & Staff Week: Mandatory training preceding the first week of camp.
camp_opening_procedures.Inventory & Risk Identification: Complete opening/closing equipment logs and examine areas for hazards.

Structuring for Zero-Friction Learning: The One at a Time Rule

songbook: (Boom Boom) Ain’t It Great To Be Crazy

necessary_paperwork: Medical Forms A, B, and C
I-9 Form and W-4 (for paid staff)
